                  About the role      Serve as the lead of our infrastructure teamOwnership of our entire infra stackAnalyze, design, develop, maintain, and improve observability & monitoring stackPush the boundaries of what is possible.Skills: Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, Python, Reflex
About the interview      The entire process is fully remote; all communication will happen via email and video chat.Once you've submitted your application, the team will review your submission and may reach out for an intro call.After the intro call, there will be a technical take-home test and then an on-site with 2-3 technical interviews, each around 45 minutes.Once the interviews are over, the team will meet to discuss several roles and candidates, and may be asked one or two follow-up questions over email, a quick call, or go directly to make an offer.

                  COMPANY OVERVIEW This is a unique opportunity to join a market-leading company in the telehealth industry. We support healthcare providers around the country through our teleradiology services. Using our novel cloud enabled medical image management and interpretation services, we help to advance patient care by facilitating rapid diagnosis from our large network of radiologists and clinical support professionals who all work from their homes. POSITION OVERVIEWThe Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) is responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining automated test frameworks that ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of our proprietary PACS and related applications. This role partners closely with software engineers to integrate automated regression testing into our development lifecycle, reducing manual testing dependency and accelerating release cycles. The SDET will build and maintain front-end, API, and system-level test coverage while supporting traceability and documentation needs appropriate for a regulated healthcare software environment. This is a hands-on engineering role requiring strong coding skills, test architecture experience, and a proactive approach to scalable quality automation. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES• Design, develop, and maintain automated test scripts and frameworks for front-end, API, and system-level components of our proprietary PACS and related applications.• Build and execute automated regression test suites to reduce manual testing effort and improve release efficiency.• Integrate automated tests into CI/CD pipelines to support continuous validation of new builds and feature updates.• Collaborate with Software Engineers and Quality Engineering to define test cases, acceptance criteria, and appropriate automation coverage.• Develop and manage reliable test data and environments to ensure repeatable and consistent validation results.• Identify defects, document findings clearly, and implement automated tests to prevent regression of resolved issues.• Support validation documentation and traceability efforts consistent with regulated healthcare software development requirements.• Contribute to ongoing improvements in test automation practices, tooling, and overall software quality processes.• Other projects and duties as assigned.JOB REQUIREMENTS AND SKILLS• Strong experience building automated tests in .NET using xUnit, NUnit, or MSTest, with assertion libraries such as FluentAssertions.• Hands-on experience with UI automation, including FlaUI for WinForms desktop applications and Playwright (.NET) for cross-browser web testing.• Experience implementing BDD practices using SpecFlow and Gherkin where appropriate to align testing with business requirements.• Proficiency integrating automated tests into CI pipelines (GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps) with solid understanding of Git workflows.• Experience testing APIs using Postman/Newman or Playwright API testing, with a strong understanding of test layering (test pyramid).• Working knowledge of test architecture patterns (e.g., Page Object Model or Screenplay), mocking frameworks (e.g., Moq or FakeItEasy), and code coverage tools such as Coverlet or SonarQube.• Familiarity with modern quality tooling such as Testcontainers for .NET, k6 for performance testing, accessibility testing tools (Accessibility Insights, axe), or OWASP ZAP for security testing in CI.• Understanding of containerized environments (Docker basics) and modern .NET ecosystems, with exposure to visual regression, observability, or performance tooling considered a plus.EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE• 3–5 years of experience in software quality engineering, with at least 2 years focused on automated testing in a .NET environment (or equivalent).• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent practical experience).• Demonstrated experience building and maintaining automated regression test suites in a CI/CD-integrated development workflow.• Experience working in an Agile or iterative software development environment in close collaboration with engineering teams.• Experience in regulated or compliance-driven environments (e.g., healthcare, medical device, or other highly controlled industries) is preferred.WORK LOCATION AND CONDITIONS• Corporate office in San Diego. Optional Hybrid Model, 3 days in the office and 2 days remote, with manager approval. • Job Type: Full-time; Exempt or Non-Exempt.REQUIRED TRAININGS• Internal Regulatory and Quality System training required for Development.PAY RANGEHourly Pay Range: $53.00 - $62.50The stated pay scale has been implemented to reward employees fairly and competitively, as well as to support recognition of employees’ career progress, ranging from entry level to experts in their field, and talent mobility. It reflects the range that StatRad reasonably expects to pay for this position at various levels of experience. The actual starting pay rate for this position will be dependent on a variety of factors, including an applicant’s relevant experience, unique skills and abilities, education, market demand, and employer business practices, and will be discussed at the offer stage with the selected candidate.

      ,
                
                  This is not a traditional software engineering role.We're building something new. At Pushnami, AI agents write 80%+ of our production code. We need someone who can make that number higher, make it faster, and make it secure. If you've shipped complex projects using Claude Code, orchestrated multiple AI coding agents simultaneously, and understand the difference between prompting for features and prompting for production-ready systems—keep reading.About the RoleSoftware development has fundamentally changed. The best engineers are no longer measured by lines of code they write—they're measured by the quality and velocity of what they ship using AI coding agents. We're looking for someone who lives this reality every day.As our AI Enablement Engineer, you'll be responsible for ensuring AI-generated code meets production standards, accelerating our entire engineering team's agentic development capabilities, and building the tools and workflows that let us move faster without sacrificing security or quality.What You'll Do• Review and quality-gate AI-generated pull requests — quickly identify when AI output is production-ready vs. needs rework, catching context window issues, missed edge cases, and incorrect assumptions• Enable the team — help engineers improve their prompting, spec writing, and AI workflow practices so the whole team ships faster• Build with AI agents — use Claude Code and similar tools to ship features yourself, demonstrating what great agentic development looks like• Own security in an AI-first environment — implement and maintain code scanning tools (Semgrep, Dependabot, etc.), establish secure workflows for AI agents accessing infrastructure, and ensure we're not trading speed for vulnerabilities• Build internal tools — create automation that makes code review faster, catches common AI mistakes, and improves our overall development velocity• Participate in architecture decisions — help shape how we structure systems so AI agents can work on them effectivelyWhat You BringRequired:• Demonstrated experience shipping complex projects using agentic AI coding tools — we want to see what you've built with Claude Code, not just that you've used ChatGPT for autocomplete• Strong code comprehension skills — ability to read and evaluate large volumes of code quickly across unfamiliar codebases• Pattern recognition for AI mistakes — you know what AI-generated code gets wrong (hallucinated APIs, context window drift, surface-level implementations)• Security awareness — understanding of common vulnerabilities, secure coding practices, and how AI agents can inadvertently introduce security risks• Proficiency in at least one of: Go, TypeScript, or Python — comfortable learning others as neededNice to Have:• Experience running multiple AI coding sessions/worktrees simultaneously• Familiarity with test-driven development using AI (TDD with AI agents)• Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, AWS CDK)• Background in lead generation, adtech, or high-volume transactional systemsWhat We're NOT Looking For• Someone who wants to write code the traditional way and views AI as optional• A pure "code reviewer" who doesn't want to ship features themselves• Someone who needs hand-holding on ambiguous problems—we need self-starters who figure things outAbout PushnamiPushnami is an AI-first performance marketing and lead generation company based in Austin, TX. We process millions of transactions daily across our Path (lead gen), Push (browser notifications), and Signals (SMS) platforms. We're a 28-person team that punches well above our weight by aggressively adopting AI across every function of the business.We're not debating whether to adopt AI. We did that in 2024. Now we're focused on being the best in the industry at AI-assisted development—and we need people who want to push that frontier with us.Compensation & Location• Salary: $90,000 - $120,000 depending on experience• Location: Austin, TX (Hybrid—in-office collaboration with flexibility)• Benefits: Health, dental, vision, 401(k), unlimited PTO• Work Authorization: Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without sponsorshipHow to ApplySend your resume along with a brief description of a complex project you've shipped using AI coding agents. Tell us what tools you used, what worked, what didn't, and what you learned. We're more interested in your approach and results than your credentials.

                  Overview:Arqaios is building ALLIE, an intelligent sensing platform embedded directly in everyday building fixtures such as light switches, power outlets, and air vents. Using mmWave radar and edge AI, ALLIE delivers real-time fall detection, occupancy awareness, gait monitoring, and energy optimization, all without cameras or wearables. It’s privacy by design, built into the walls.We’re hiring a Senior Firmware Engineer to own the embedded systems that power ALLIE. You’ll develop the firmware that drives our sensing pipeline, integrate hardware and software at the lowest levels, and ensure our devices perform reliably in production environments. You’ll work shoulder-to-shoulder with our AI/ML team and product leadership to ship a product that people trust with their safety.This is a foundational engineering role at a startup with real traction, patented technology, and a clear path to market. If you want to build something that matters, from the silicon up, this is it.What You'll Do:Firmware Development & Edge Processing      Design, develop, and optimize embedded firmware in C/C++ for ALLIE’s sensing fixtures, including radar initialization, ADC acquisition, FFT processing, and ML inference at the edgeImplement and maintain the full signal processing pipelineDevelop OTA firmware update mechanisms that allow remote deployment without physical fixture access
Hardware Integration & Bring-Up      Lead sensor bring-up and configuration for mmWave radar chipsets and environmental sensorsOwn hardware–firmware integration across UART, SPI, I2C, and powerline communication interfacesConfigure and debug microcontrollers and SoCs (ESP32, ARM-based MCUs), including peripherals, memory, and timing behaviorRead and interpret hardware schematics, datasheets, and reference designs to drive integration decisionsCollaborate with PCB design partners on Gen2 fixture design and implementation
Wireless Connectivity & IoT Protocols:      Design and implement secure wireless communication stacks using Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) on ESP32-C6 and similar platforms, ensuring reliable, low-latency data transport between fixtures, hubs, and cloud servicesLead the integration of Matter and Thread protocols to position ALLIE fixtures as interoperable nodes within the broader smart home ecosystem (Apple Home, Google Home, Amazon Alexa)Implement and validate Thread mesh networking for low-power, reliable fixture-to-fixture and fixture-to-hub communication in dense building environmentsEvaluate, prototype, and integrate additional wireless protocols (BLE 5.x, Zigbee, Z-Wave) as needed for third-party device interoperability and ecosystem expansionArchitect hybrid connectivity strategies combining wireless protocols with powerline communication for maximum reliability in steel-framed commercial buildings and multi-unit residential facilitiesWork with the product team to ensure wireless implementations meet Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA) certification requirements for Matter and Thread complianceOptimize wireless stack performance for power consumption, range, throughput, and coexistence
Hub / Gateway Platform Development:      Develop and maintain embedded Linux software for ALLIE’s facility hub/gateway running on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, Raspberry Pi, or similar SBC platformsImplement event ingestion and multi-fixture data fusion logic, aggregating alerts from up to 50 fixtures per facility, consolidating overlapping detections into single actionable eventsBuild and optimize local ML inference pipelines on the hub using GPU-accelerated frameworks (NVIDIA TensorRT, CUDA) for advanced analytics that exceed fixture-level edge processing capabilitiesDesign and implement a resilient local event queue with 24-hour offline buffering to ensure no data loss during cloud connectivity interruptions
Testing, validation & Reliability:      Design and execute comprehensive testing and validation strategies for firmware and hardware integrationDiagnose and resolve system-level issues related to timing, throughput, stability, and false alarm ratesDevelop Python-based tools for automated testing, data inspection, and integration workflowsSupport regulatory certification efforts (FCC Part 15.255, UL/ETL) by providing firmware-level test artifacts and compliance documentation
Collaboration & Architecture:      Partner closely with AI/ML engineers to ensure firmware outputs are reliable and compatible with downstream ML models.Work with product managers to translate system and product requirements into firmware-level implementationsContribute to system architecture discussions with emphasis on scalability, maintainability, and the interplay between hardware and software development schedulesDocument firmware design decisions, configuration assumptions, and testing results to support a growing engineering team
Required Qualifications:      Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field AND 3+ years of embedded firmware development experience, OR equivalent practical experienceStrong proficiency in C and/or C++ for embedded systems developmentHands-on experience working with MCUs or SoCs in real hardware environments (ESP32, STM32, or similar ARM-based platforms)Experience with vendor SDKs and development frameworks such as ESP-IDF, STM32 HAL/LL, or FreeRTOSWorking experience with embedded communication protocols, including UART, SPI, and I2CProficiency in Python for testing, automation, and integration tasksAbility to read and interpret datasheets, reference manuals, and hardware schematicsExperience collaborating with cross-functional teams, including AI/ML engineers and product managersStrong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment
Preferred Qualifications:      Master’s Degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or related field AND 3+ years of embedded systems experience OR Bachelor’s Degree AND 5+ years equivalent experienceExperience with mmWave radar devices or similar RF sensing platformsUnderstanding of radar signal processing concepts: FMCW, range-FFT, Doppler-FFT, CFAR detection, point cloud generation, clustering, and trackingExperience integrating embedded firmware outputs with ML/AI pipelines and data processing workflowsFamiliarity with RTOS or bare-metal firmware development patternsExperience with secure embedded wireless communication using Wi-Fi 6, Thread, and Matter protocols for IoT interoperabilityWorking knowledge of firmware flashing, bootloaders, and OTA update mechanismsExperience with powerline communication or non-standard embedded networkingLinux-based development and cross-compilation experienceFamiliarity with ML/data processing libraries (NumPy, SciPy, PyTorch, TensorFlow) for testing and validationPrior experience in a startup or early-stage product environment where you wore multiple hats
Compensation and Work ArrangementThe base salary range will be commensurate with the candidate's experience and qualifications. Compensation may also include equity participation in our early-stage team.Work Arrangement: Full-time position. Remote-friendly with regular collaboration across engineering and product teams. Periodic on-site work in Chicago may be required for hardware integration and testing milestones.Visa Sponsorship: Arqaios welcomes international talent. We support candidates on STEM OPT, OPT, and H-1B visas, and we sponsor H-1B transfers and new petitions for qualified candidates.What we offer:      Ownership of critical firmware and hardware components in a real-world, award-winning productDirect collaboration with AI engineers, product leadership, and the founding teamOpportunity to influence system architecture and technical direction from the ground floorWork on technically challenging problems at the intersection of radar, edge AI, and IoTVisa sponsorship (STEM OPT, OPT, H-1B)Growth opportunities as the product scales from pilots to commercial launch across multiple verticals
